perturbation.justification: The changed text removes Merck KGaA as an 'Additional Reporting Person', which is explicitly listed as a party in the contradicted text. This omission contradicts the joint filing agreement's understanding of multiple reporting persons and the signatures from 'MERCK KGAA, DARMSTADT, GERMANY' on the document, thus creating an in-text contradiction as described under the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: MERCK SERONO SA, COINSINS, SWITZERLAND, AN AFFILIATE OF MERCK KGAA, DARMSTADT, GERMANY By: /s/ Cédric Hyde By: /s/ Tearaboth Te Name: Cédric Hyde Name: Tearaboth Te Title: CFO Title: Treasury Director

perturbation.explanation: By removing one of the signatures of Tearaboth Te, Title: Treasury Director, this creates an in-text contradiction as it removes a signing authority for that party. The document now lacks the full representation initially intended, which affects the enforcement and the understanding of the agreement, due to the lack of the additional representant of the company. The document originally designated two signing authorities, indicating dual control, but the modification concentrates authority on a single individual, Cédric Hyde, in the signature block. This directly contradicts the document's initial structure that presumed a dual-authorized signature for that entity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: MERCK SERONO SA, COINSINS, SWITZERLAND, AN AFFILIATE OF MERCK KGAA, DARMSTADT, GERMANY By: /s/ Cédric Hyde Name: Cédric Hyde Title: CFO MERCK KGAA, DARMSTADT, GERMANY MERCK KGAA, DARMSTADT, GERMANY By: /s/ Rando Bruns By: /s/ Tim Nielsen Name: Rando Bruns Name: Tim Nielsen Title: Head of Treasury Title: Head of Capital Markets

This contradicts the implied requirement in the contradicted text, which assumes dual authorization from MERCK SERONO SA. The contradicted text shows other entities having two signatories and by removing a signatory from the Changed Text, it creates an in-text contradiction.
